guidance.cap-edr: EDR tools monitor endpoint activity in real time. The Responsible role deploys and tunes the tool; the ISSO validates it covers all CUI-bearing endpoints for 3.14.6/3.14.7.
guidance.cap-av: Anti-malware must run on all endpoints and update signatures automatically. The Responsible role manages deployment and update schedules per 3.14.2.
guidance.cap-mdm: MDM enforces device compliance — encryption, PIN, remote wipe — for mobile devices accessing organizational resources per 3.1.18/3.1.19.
guidance.cap-device-encryption: Full-disk encryption using FIPS 140-2/-3 validated modules is required on all endpoints storing CUI. Attach FIPS cert numbers to the SSP per 3.13.10.
guidance.cap-iam: IAM covers the full lifecycle: provisioning, access reviews, and deprovisioning. The Responsible role runs access reviews; HR is consulted on terminations per 3.1.1/3.1.2.
guidance.cap-mfa: MFA is required for all non-local, non-privileged and privileged access per 3.5.3. The Responsible role configures and enforces MFA policies across all authentication systems.
guidance.cap-pam: PAM restricts privileged account use — shared admin accounts are a common assessment finding. The Responsible role manages just-in-time access and privileged session logging per 3.1.6/3.1.7.
guidance.cap-directory: Active Directory and Entra ID are often the single point of identity for CMMC scope. The Responsible role manages group policy, OUs, and federation settings per 3.5.1/3.5.2.
guidance.cap-vuln-scan: Authenticated vulnerability scans of all in-scope systems are required per 3.11.2. Results must be tracked and remediated within defined SLAs. The ISSO typically owns the scan cadence and remediation reporting.
guidance.cap-pentest: Penetration testing is not explicitly required by CMMC L2, but is evidence of proactive risk management under 3.11.2. Legal is consulted to ensure proper scoping agreements are in place.
guidance.cap-patch: Patch management is one of the most common assessment findings. Define SLAs (e.g., critical = 30 days), track compliance, and document exceptions. Multiple Responsible roles are typical for different system types per 3.14.1/3.14.4/3.14.5.
guidance.cap-firewall: Firewall rule reviews should be documented and periodic. The Responsible role maintains baseline rule sets and approves all changes per 3.13.1/3.13.5.
guidance.cap-segmentation: CUI systems must be isolated from general IT traffic. Assess whether the authorization boundary matches actual network segmentation per 3.13.3/3.13.4.
guidance.cap-vpn: Remote access to CUI systems must traverse a managed VPN with MFA. The Responsible role configures split tunneling policy and session timeouts per 3.1.12.
guidance.cap-wireless: If wireless networks are in scope, WPA3 and network isolation are required. Guest networks must be completely separated from CUI segments per 3.13.15.
guidance.cap-dns: DNS filtering (e.g., blocking malicious domains) is increasingly expected by assessors as a protective technology per 3.13.5. The Responsible role manages DNS policies.
guidance.cap-cloud-infra: Cloud IaaS/PaaS requires CSP CRM alignment — which controls are inherited vs. customer-responsible. The Cloud Admin is Responsible for implementing customer responsibilities per the CSP CRM.
guidance.cap-saas: SaaS platforms like M365 GCC High, Salesforce, etc. require admin configuration hardening, DLP policies, and periodic access reviews per 3.4.1/3.4.2.
guidance.cap-casb: CASB provides visibility into shadow IT and controls data egress to unapproved cloud services. Often a shared responsibility between ISSO and Cloud Admin per 3.1.3.
guidance.cap-cloud-backup: Cloud backups of CUI-bearing systems must be encrypted and stored in geo-redundant locations. Recovery testing should be documented per 3.8.9.
guidance.cap-data-class: CUI marking and handling is foundational to CMMC scope. The ISSO owns the data classification program; Legal and HR are consulted on policy and training per 3.8.1/3.8.2.
guidance.cap-dlp: DLP controls prevent unauthorized exfiltration of CUI via email, USB, and cloud uploads. The ISSO tunes policies; Legal reviews data handling rules per 3.1.3/3.13.1.
guidance.cap-encrypt-rest: All CUI at rest — databases, file servers, workstations, backups — must use FIPS 140-2/-3 validated encryption. Multiple Responsible roles cover different system types per 3.13.10/3.13.16.
guidance.cap-encrypt-transit: All CUI in transit must use TLS 1.2+ with FIPS-validated cipher suites. The Network Engineer owns transport layer; Cloud Admin owns cloud transit per 3.13.8/3.13.11.
guidance.cap-media: Media sanitization requires documented procedures and, for high-sensitivity assets, third-party certificates of destruction. The ISSO owns policy; SysAdmin executes per 3.8.3/3.8.4.
guidance.cap-siem: SIEM aggregates logs from all CUI-handling systems and generates alerts. The ISSO owns the SIEM; contributing teams forward logs per 3.3.1/3.3.2.
guidance.cap-logging: Every CUI-handling system must generate and forward logs to a central collector. Retention must meet contractual and regulatory minimums. Multiple Responsible roles are expected per 3.3.1/3.3.5.
guidance.cap-secops: Day-to-day security operations include alert triage, threat hunting, and escalation. This is typically the ISSO or a dedicated SecOps analyst per 3.6.2/3.14.6.
guidance.cap-ir-plan: The incident response plan must identify roles, escalation procedures, and notification timelines (including DIBCAC notification per DFARS 252.204-7012). Multiple stakeholders are Consulted per 3.6.1/3.6.2.
guidance.cap-ir-handle: When an incident occurs, multiple technical teams are Responsible for containment and eradication. The ISSO leads coordination; Legal is consulted on notifications per 3.6.1.
guidance.cap-bcdr: BC/DR ensures the organization can recover from a disruption within documented RTO/RPO targets. Multiple technical teams are Responsible for system recovery per 3.11.1.
guidance.cap-policy: The information security policy must be reviewed and approved at least annually per 3.12.4. The ISSO drafts; Legal reviews; CISO approves.
guidance.cap-risk: A formal risk assessment must be conducted at least annually and whenever significant changes occur per 3.11.1. The ISSO leads; all technical leads contribute.
guidance.cap-training: All personnel with access to CUI must complete security awareness training at least annually per 3.2.1/3.2.2. HR administers; ISSO develops content.
guidance.cap-cmmc: CMMC assessment preparation requires coordination across all technical and governance teams. The ISSO leads evidence collection; CISO is accountable to the C3PAO per 3.12.1/3.12.4.
guidance.cap-supply-chain: Vendors and subcontractors who process CUI must flow CMMC requirements down per DFARS. Legal owns contract language; ISSO reviews security posture per 3.11.1/3.11.3.
guidance.cap-config-mgmt: Configuration baselines must be established and deviations must go through a formal change control process per 3.4.1/3.4.5. Multiple technical teams are Responsible for their systems.
guidance.cap-physical-access: Physical access to areas where CUI is processed must be controlled and logged per 3.10.1/3.10.2. Facilities/IT Director typically owns physical security.
guidance.cap-facility: Facility security includes perimeter controls, cameras, and environmental monitoring. The IT Director owns technical facility controls; HR owns personnel access policies per 3.10.1/3.10.3.
guidance.cap-equipment: Decommissioned hardware must be sanitized (NIST SP 800-88) before disposal. The ISSO owns policy; SysAdmin executes. Certificates of destruction are evidence per 3.8.3/3.10.5.
